body.title: agent_datacenter: skeleton flat-file device registry — atomic write, ping-on-restart
body.result: DeviceRegistry in skeleton/registry.py: atomic write (tmp→rename), ping-on-restart, idempotent register, status tracking. No Postgres dep. All tests pass.
body.description:
The skeleton's device registry must NOT depend on Postgres (skeleton manages Postgres). It uses a flat JSON file with atomic writes.

Problem: If registry used Postgres, skeleton couldn't restart Postgres when Postgres goes down — circular dependency. Flat file breaks the cycle.

Proposed shape:
- agent_datacenter/skeleton/registry.py:
  class DeviceRegistry:
    _path: Path  # ~/.agent_datacenter/devices.json
    def register(self, device_id: str, config: DeviceConfig, mailbox: str) -> None
    def deregister(self, device_id: str) -> None
    def list_devices(self) -> list[dict]
    def get_device(self, device_id: str) -> dict | None
    def _atomic_write(self, data: dict) -> None:  # write to .tmp, rename
    def _load(self) -> dict
  On skeleton restart:
    read file → for each device: ping health endpoint → mark online/offline
  Device record: {id, name, status: 'online'|'offline'|'blocked', mailbox, config: DeviceConfig, registered_at: ISO8601}

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/skeleton/registry.py

Design rules:
- oop-first: DeviceRegistry class with clear methods — no module-level state
- no-sqlite: JSON flat file only — no sqlite3 import allowed
- docs-in-code: module docstring explains the circular-dep reason for flat-file choice

Scope boundary:
IN: flat-file registry CRUD, atomic write, ping-on-restart reattach loop
OUT: health rollup (T-adc-rack-health-rollup), MCP aggregator (T-adc-skeleton-mcp-aggregator)

Test plan:
- register() writes to file; list_devices() reads it back correctly
- Atomic write: simulate crash mid-write (truncate .tmp) — no corruption to main file
- Ping-on-restart: mock health endpoint returns 200 → device marked online; timeout → offline
- Re-running register() with same device_id updates record (idempotent)
